content.content:
700	### CDC / Change Tracking
701	
702	| Use case | Status | Notes |
703	|----------|--------|-------|
704	| Notify on create | ✅ `type: 'create'` | |
705	| Notify on update (with prev value) | ✅ `type: 'update'`, `prev` always provided | |
706	| Notify on delete (with prev value) | ✅ `type: 'delete'`, `prev` always provided | |
707	| Notify on any change | ✅ Handle all three event types in one handler | |
708	| Conditional CDC (only fire if a specific field changed) | ❌ Handler-space concern | Check `event.prev.field !== event.entry.field` yourself |
709	| Declarative `watches` on kit export | ❌ Deferred | Imperative `watch()` in apparatus `start()` covers the use case |
710	| Async CDC (non-blocking, queued) | ❌ Out of scope for Stacks | Clockworks handles async dispatch at a higher level |
711	| CDC ordering guarantees across handlers | ✅ Registration order | Deterministic; depends on plugin load order |
712	| CDC for batch operations | ❌ Out of scope v1 | Fires once per document when batch ops ship |
713	
714	### Cross-Plugin Access
715	
716	| Use case | Status | Notes |
717	|----------|--------|-------|
718	| Read from own books | ✅ `stacks.book(myId, 'name')` | Full read/write |
719	| Read from another plugin's books | ✅ `stacks.readBook(otherId, 'name')` | Read-only by type |
720	| Write to another plugin's books | ❌ Out of scope | Use the owning plugin's tools |
721	
722	### Schema Lifecycle
723	
724	| Use case | Status | Notes |
725	|----------|--------|-------|
726	| Create a new book | ✅ Kit `books` contribution | Additive only — picked up by startup reconciliation |
727	| Add an index to an existing book | ✅ Kit `books` contribution | Additive only — `ensureBook` is idempotent and creates new indexes on next boot |
728	| Retire a book (drop the underlying storage) | ✅ `stacks.dropBook(ownerId, bookName)` | Imperative, top-level only (refused inside `transaction(...)`); fires one Phase-2 `delete-book` event; idempotent on missing books |
729	| Drop an index without dropping the book | ❌ Out of scope v1 | No use case has earned this surface; ship `dropBook` first, layer index retirement later if real demand appears |
730	| Migrate row data between books before retirement | ❌ Caller-space | If preservation is required, read rows out before calling `dropBook`; the substrate does not migrate on drop |
731	| Retire a book by removing its kit declaration | ❌ Permanently out of scope | Kit reconciliation is additive only; removing a `books` entry from a kit declaration silently leaves the storage in place. The sanctioned retirement path is the explicit imperative `dropBook` call from an apparatus's `start()` |
732	
733	---
734	
735	## 8. Backend Interface
736	
737	The `StacksBackend` interface is the only persistence contract. All SQLite-specific types stay behind this interface — `better-sqlite3`, raw SQL strings, and JSONPath expressions are implementation details of the SQLite adapter, not visible to the apparatus or to plugins.
738	
739	> **Async vs. sync signatures.** The signatures below use `Promise` return types as the general contract — a future backend (e.g. libSQL over HTTP, a networked store) may require asynchronous I/O. The current `better-sqlite3` implementation is fully synchronous: all methods return plain values, not promises. Both shapes satisfy the interface; callers should `await` regardless so they remain backend-agnostic.
740	
741	```typescript
742	/**
743	 * Persistence abstraction for The Stacks.
744	 *
745	 * The default implementation uses SQLite (better-sqlite3). Alternative
746	 * implementations (e.g. in-memory for tests, libSQL for edge deployments)
747	 * implement this interface.
748	 *
749	 * The apparatus never depends on the concrete adapter type — only this interface.

file.content:
### CDC / Change Tracking

| Use case | Status | Notes |
|----------|--------|-------|
| Notify on create | ✅ `type: 'create'` | |
| Notify on update (with prev value) | ✅ `type: 'update'`, `prev` always provided | |
| Notify on delete (with prev value) | ✅ `type: 'delete'`, `prev` always provided | |
| Notify on any change | ✅ Handle all three event types in one handler | |
| Conditional CDC (only fire if a specific field changed) | ❌ Handler-space concern | Check `event.prev.field !== event.entry.field` yourself |
| Declarative `watches` on kit export | ❌ Deferred | Imperative `watch()` in apparatus `start()` covers the use case |
| Async CDC (non-blocking, queued) | ❌ Out of scope for Stacks | Clockworks handles async dispatch at a higher level |
| CDC ordering guarantees across handlers | ✅ Registration order | Deterministic; depends on plugin load order |
| CDC for batch operations | ❌ Out of scope v1 | Fires once per document when batch ops ship |

### Cross-Plugin Access

| Use case | Status | Notes |
|----------|--------|-------|
| Read from own books | ✅ `stacks.book(myId, 'name')` | Full read/write |
| Read from another plugin's books | ✅ `stacks.readBook(otherId, 'name')` | Read-only by type |
| Write to another plugin's books | ❌ Out of scope | Use the owning plugin's tools |

### Schema Lifecycle

| Use case | Status | Notes |
|----------|--------|-------|
| Create a new book | ✅ Kit `books` contribution | Additive only — picked up by startup reconciliation |
| Add an index to an existing book | ✅ Kit `books` contribution | Additive only — `ensureBook` is idempotent and creates new indexes on next boot |
| Retire a book (drop the underlying storage) | ✅ `stacks.dropBook(ownerId, bookName)` | Imperative, top-level only (refused inside `transaction(...)`); fires one Phase-2 `delete-book` event; idempotent on missing books |
| Drop an index without dropping the book | ❌ Out of scope v1 | No use case has earned this surface; ship `dropBook` first, layer index retirement later if real demand appears |
| Migrate row data between books before retirement | ❌ Caller-space | If preservation is required, read rows out before calling `dropBook`; the substrate does not migrate on drop |
| Retire a book by removing its kit declaration | ❌ Permanently out of scope | Kit reconciliation is additive only; removing a `books` entry from a kit declaration silently leaves the storage in place. The sanctioned retirement path is the explicit imperative `dropBook` call from an apparatus's `start()` |

---

## 8. Backend Interface

The `StacksBackend` interface is the only persistence contract. All SQLite-specific types stay behind this interface — `better-sqlite3`, raw SQL strings, and JSONPath expressions are implementation details of the SQLite adapter, not visible to the apparatus or to plugins.

> **Async vs. sync signatures.** The signatures below use `Promise` return types as the general contract — a future backend (e.g. libSQL over HTTP, a networked store) may require asynchronous I/O. The current `better-sqlite3` implementation is fully synchronous: all methods return plain values, not promises. Both shapes satisfy the interface; callers should `await` regardless so they remain backend-agnostic.

```typescript
/**
 * Persistence abstraction for The Stacks.
 *
 * The default implementation uses SQLite (better-sqlite3). Alternative
 * implementations (e.g. in-memory for tests, libSQL for edge deployments)
 * implement this interface.
 *
 * The apparatus never depends on the concrete adapter type — only this interface.

packages/plugins/stacks/docs/specification.md:36:Kits declare the books they own via a `books` contribution field. The Stacks reads this at startup and creates or reconciles the necessary indexes. **Startup-time schema reconciliation is additive only** — kit contributions cannot remove a book or index, so simply dropping a book from a kit declaration leaves the abandoned table on every existing on-disk database. The sanctioned retirement path is the explicit imperative `StacksApi.dropBook` (§3); it is **never invoked implicitly from kit declarations**. New books and new indexes from a kit declaration are always safe; nothing is ever dropped automatically by the reconciliation pass.

113	  // Lifecycle observation is a row-level concern. Both `delete`

115	  // are no-ops here — neither carries a writ entry and the writs book
116	  // is never dropped at runtime today.

118

120

122	  // patches (title rename, codex inheritance, etc.) must not produce
123	  // lifecycle events. For create events the "delta" is the writ entering
124	  // existence at its initial phase.
125	  if (event.type === 'update') {
126	    if (writ.phase === event.prev.phase) return;

content.text:
### Overall: PASS

### Completeness

All 8 tasks (t1–t8) and 16 decisions (D1–D16) in the spec are addressed:

| Spec area | Status |
|---|---|
| t1 — `BookDeleteEvent` variant on `ChangeEvent<T>` | ✅ `types.ts:143-153` |
| t2 — `StacksBackend.dropBook` + both impls | ✅ `backend.ts:91`, `memory-backend.ts:242-247`, `sqlite-backend.ts:350-357` |
| t3 — `StacksApi.dropBook` with tx guard + Phase-2 fire | ✅ `stacks-core.ts:414-438` |
| t4 — Updated CDC consumers | ✅ `lattice.ts:373`, `writ-lifecycle-observer.ts:117` (others use `event.type !== X` early-return — already covers the new variant) |
| t5 — Bridge translation + carve-out | ✅ `clockworks-stacks-signals.ts:109-117, 143-171` |
| t6 — Tier 1/2/4 conformance tests | ✅ Tests 1.12, 2.30, 2.31, 4.12, 4.13, 4.14, 4.15 |
| t7 — Cartograph retro-cleanup | ✅ `cartograph.ts:792-812` (sequential, before `registerWritType`) |
| t8 — Doc updates in lockstep | ✅ All four files updated; "additive only" nuanced in three locations with consistent wording |

§7 use-case table has the new "Schema Lifecycle" section. Conformance catalogue lists all new tests under their assigned tiers. Acceptance signal #4 (on-disk SQLite verification) is covered indirectly: the cartograph test asserts the three calls happen via a recording proxy, and Tier 1.12 against `SqliteBackend` proves `dropBook` actually drops the table.

### Correctness

- `pnpm -w typecheck` passes across all 24 projects.
- `pnpm -w test` passes — 198 stacks tests (against both backends), 14 bridge tests, 94 cartograph tests, plus the rest of the workspace. Total ~3500+ tests, 0 failures.
- Phase-2 depth tracking is wrapped around the `delete-book` fire (`stacks-core.ts:430-437`), preserving the cross-transaction re-entry bound for handlers that open transactions on drop.
- Carve-out predicate runs at watcher registration, so `clockworks/events` is naturally excluded from `book-dropped` echoes (verified by recursion-guard test).

One non-blocking observation: `sqlite-backend.ts` also gained a `bindScalar` boolean→integer coercion (lines 62-71, 88-119). This is unrelated to `dropBook` per se but is required for Tier 4.6 (boolean type fidelity) to pass against `SqliteBackend` — which only became live with this commission's new `conformance.sqlite.test.ts` entry point. Reasonable to include here since the spec demands the suite pass against both backends.

### Quality

- Comments cite decision numbers (D1, D2, …) at every load-bearing site, matching the existing codebase convention.
- The cartograph retro-cleanup test uses a recording proxy rather than peeking at SQLite tables, which keeps the test in-process and aligned with existing fixture patterns.
- Both backends' `dropBook` implementations are minimal (no explicit index drops, no transaction machinery) per D7/D8.
- Error message in 4.14 ("transaction is active") is precise and points operators at the fix.
- Docstrings on `StacksApi.dropBook` and `StacksBackend.dropBook` are duplicated across `types.ts` / `backend.ts` and the spec markdown — intentional to keep the spec self-contained, consistent with how the existing `transaction()` is documented.

### Required Changes

None. Ship it.
The review passed with no required changes. Let me confirm the working state and exit cleanly.
